justification: B correctly classifies all three projects as 'Embedded Systems / IoT / Robotics' and captures concrete impact highlights including the 2nd place award, ~30% water reduction metric, and 50Hz control loop performance. A extracts no projects and no impact highlights at all, scoring poorly on those two dimensions. On skills, A adds inferred domain labels ('Embedded Systems', 'Firmware Development', 'Sensor Fusion', 'Real-time Operating Systems') that are reasonable but slightly inflated as standalone skills, while B incorrectly excludes Python (which is self-reported but legitimately listed) and FreeRTOS (which is listed in skills and relevant to the RTOS project). Both have minor skill accuracy issues, so they tie on that dimension. On conservatism, A's 14 skills is slightly bloated with domain abstractions, while B's 10 is reasonable but drops a few legitimate ones (FreeRTOS, GDB, Python), making both roughly equivalent. The decisive advantage for B is its complete project classification and rich, metric-backed impact highlights versus A's total absence of both.
